L1.area: Memory ownership and element transfer operations for collection and array types

L2.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in bulk element transfer logic where raw pointer reads are used to take ownership of elements while invoking a caller-provided closure. If the closure panics, elements that have already been moved out of the source container are dropped again during stack unwinding, resulting in a double free and potential memory corruption.

L3.area: Slice move source trait implementations for array-backed and boxed-slice containers
L3.description: A use-after-free vulnerability exists in the bulk element move operations for array-backed and heap-allocated slice sources. Raw pointer reads are used to take ownership of each element while invoking a caller-provided closure, but the source container's drop behavior is not inhibited until after all elements are consumed. If the closure panics during element processing, elements whose ownership has already been transferred are dropped again during unwinding, resulting in a double free.

ground_truth.reason: The SliceMoveSource and SliceMoveSourceCollection trait implementations for arrays and boxed slices use ptr::read to duplicate element ownership without inhibiting drops on the source, causing a double free if the user-provided closure panics during unwinding.
